Title: Symplasmic phloem loading and subcellular transport in storage roots are key factors for carbon allocation in cassava

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon storage and osmotic regulation during storage root bulking
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In storage root, all four MeTST1/2 increased during bulking (cluster SR4); MeTST3b increased throughout bulking (cluster SR1)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling across developmental stages; non-aqueous fractionation sugar compartmentation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased vacuolar accumulation of soluble sugars during storage root bulking
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (SRA BioProject PRJNA1045511); metabolite profiling; non-aqueous fractionation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeTST tonoplast importers were strongly induced during cassava storage root bulking. 2. Their expression pattern matched increased soluble sugar accumulation in storage roots. 3. NAF supported predominant sugar compartmentation into vacuoles.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate targets to alter soluble sugar storage and sink strength in cassava storage roots; should be balanced against starch accumulation tradeoffs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced vacuolar sugar sequestration may compete with carbon flux to starch/dry matter
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TST1/2 can transport sucrose as well as hexoses according to orthology-based inference.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sucrose transporter; MST family hexose transporters
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts decreased throughout storage root bulking (clusters SR2/SR3)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression is associated with retention/accumulation of sugars in storage root vacuoles during bulking
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Vacuolar sugar exporters declined during root bulking. 2. This expression trend opposed that of vacuolar importers. 3. The pattern supports sugar sequestration rather than export in bulking roots.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential targets to increase sugar retention in storage roots
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Restricting export may alter cytosolic sugar availability and starch biosynthesis balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assignment based on orthology to AtSUC4/ERD6-type tonoplast exporters.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports vacuolar energization for sugar storage and osmotic regulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Four of five MeAVP1 and 26 of 35 MeV-ATPase genes increased in storage root clusters SR1/SR4
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced vacuole acidification/energization accompanying sugar accumulation during root bulking
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Vacuolar proton pump machinery was induced during storage root bulking. 2. This supports secondary active transport into the vacuole. 3. The trend coincided with increased sugar compartmentation in vacuoles.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targets to modulate vacuolar sink capacity and osmotic storage
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing vacuolar sink strength may divert carbon away from starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes support transport energetics rather than catalyze terminal metabolite synthesis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon reserve in cassava storage roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All four GPT genes sharply increased at during-bulking in storage root (cluster SR4)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong induction in bulking storage roots
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq developmental profiling; physiological interpretation from starch accumulation data
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports increased carbon import into amyloplasts for starch biosynthesis during root bulking
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. All cassava GPT orthologs rose sharply during storage root bulking. 2. GPT likely increases G6P supply to amyloplasts. 3. This supports elevated starch biosynthesis in bulking roots.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve starch deposition and dry matter in cassava roots
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Greater plastid import may reduce soluble sugar pools used for osmotic functions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transporter classified here as Common Gene, not pathway enzyme.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon reserve in cassava storage roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One of two NTT orthologs was co-expressed with GPT orthologs during bulking
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in storage root during bulking
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ADP/ATP exchange linked to amyloplast starch biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A cassava NTT ortholog was co-induced with GPT genes during bulking. 2. This supports energetic coupling of ADP-glucose utilization for starch synthesis. 3. The transporter likely contributes to amyloplast carbon storage capacity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to strengthen starch biosynthetic capacity in roots
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May require coordinated engineering with starch pathway enzymes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative transcript co-expression, not direct functional perturbation in cassava.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Water-associated osmotic solute accumulation linked to sugars
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Facilitates water influx accompanying osmotic sugar storage in bulking roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: 9 of 13 expressed PIPs and 5 of 9 expressed TIPs increased during root bulking
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced water transport capacity during osmotic expansion of storage roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple plasma membrane and tonoplast aquaporins increased during root bulking. 2. Their induction coincided with rising vacuolar sugar accumulation. 3. They likely facilitate water influx needed for storage parenchyma expansion.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential targets to modify water storage and bulking physiology
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect drought responses and tissue water content independently of dry matter
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not direct nutrient metabolism genes, but core to storage root carbon/water allocation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose cleavage and sink carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Symplasmic unloading-associated sucrose utilization in secondary vasculature and storage root
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves sucrose with UDP to form UDP-glucose and fructose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and sink strength for starch/cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon storage in storage roots; support of sink metabolism and cell wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeSUS1a highest in wood and adult storage root; increased at DB in storage root and decreased in bark; MeSUS1b similar but weaker
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in secondary vasculature, especially wood and bulking storage root
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key sink-strength-associated step, not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq developmental profiling; comparison with NSC/starch levels; prior promoter studies referenced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports carbon availability and sink strength in bulking storage root; MeSUS1a especially marks shift in sink strength from bark to storage root
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeSUS1a and MeSUS1b are highly expressed in cassava secondary vasculature. 2. MeSUS1a increases strongly in bulking storage roots while decreasing in bark, consistent with reallocation of sink strength. 3. The genes likely support symplasmic unloading-associated carbon supply rather than directly tracking starch levels.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate genes for improving sink strength and root filling; may be useful in gene-stacking with starch biosynthesis genes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter partitioning between storage root and stem/wood; may affect secondary cell wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors highlight MeSUS1 genes as particularly interesting research targets.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose cleavage linked to starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves sucrose with UDP to generate UDP-glucose and fructose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon reserve in storage roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in storage root and bark, also wood; transcript levels in wood and storage root tightly followed relative starch content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq developmental profiling correlated with tissue starch levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes specifically to starch biosynthesis in bulking storage root
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeSUS3a expression was high in storage root, bark, and wood. 2. In wood and storage root, transcript abundance closely tracked starch levels. 3. This pattern implies a role in starch biosynthesis distinct from the more general sink role of MeSUS1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance starch deposition in cassava roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May modify carbon balance across woody and storage tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inference is based on correlation with starch content, not direct gene perturbation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Photosynthetic sucrose production and sink sucrose cycling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes sucrose-6-phosphate from UDP-glucose and fructose-6-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports sucrose production and cycling that feeds sink metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary transported carbon form and sink substrate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeSPSA increased in bark and storage root and other tissues; MeSPSB/C transcripts were much more abundant in source leaf
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-enriched SPSB/C; broader induction of SPSA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indicates tissue-specific sucrose synthesis/cycling, with SPSB/C linked to photosynthetic sucrose production and SPSA to sink sucrose cycling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental progression from pre-bulking to during-bulking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeSPSA genes increased in bark and storage root and also in other tissues. 2. MeSPSB/C were much more abundant in source leaves, suggesting a photosynthetic role. 3. The data support active sucrose cycling in sink organs and distinct SPS isoform specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential targets to tune source–sink sucrose metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing sucrose cycling may influence both transport efficiency and starch deposition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors interpret SPSA induction as compatible with common sucrose cycling in sink organs.
